title: spt-core runtime manifest
description: Per-adapter runtime manifest for the spt-core harness contract. Authored as TOML (this schema describes the equivalent data model). A manifest declares only what varies per harness/shell; command templates are opaque strings spt-core never parses. Cross-field invariants (kind<->[shell] agreement, strategy/avenue field requirements) are enforced by spt-core's validate step beyond this schema.

hooks.description: `[hooks.<event>]` — inbound hook table, keyed by harness event name.

profiles.description: `[profiles.<name>]` — **shipped** profile overlays: sparse leaf-replace overlays declared by the adapter dev inside the parent manifest, updating as one unit with it. Stored raw ([`toml::Value`]); [`crate::profile::resolve`] merges one onto the base and re-validates the complete manifest. A bare `adapter_name` ignores these (parent unmodified); the composite `<adapter>:<profile>` selects one. **Local** (node-local, user-authored) profiles live beside the adapter in the registry, never here. (CONTEXT.md §adapter profile.)

strings.description: `[strings]` — an adapter-authored KV tree of **opaque data** (spt-core never executes a string; command templates live in their own sections behind registration). Dot-path-readable via `spt adapter get-string`, and it rides the same leaf-replace profile overlay as the rest of the manifest (a shipped or local profile may override base strings). Node-local; no cross-node sync. (CONTEXT.md §adapter strings.)

hints.description: `[[hints]]` — once-per-session keyword hints (CONTEXT.md §keyword hints). **Order is significant** (first match wins). A profile overlays this by leaf-replace like any section — the array is replaced wholesale, never spliced (override/extend = re-declare).

Adapter.description: `[adapter]` — the manifest header, readable before any update (compat gate).

min_spt_core_version.description: Lowest spt-core version this adapter tolerates (compat gate).

hostable_types.description: Endpoint types this adapter can host (`LiveAgent`, `Worker`, …).

shortcut_basename.description: Optional basename for the `spt endpoint run` picker's `<basename>-<id>` launcher shortcut (REQ-MANIFEST-7). Absent ⇒ the harness-agnostic default `spt` (→ `spt-<id>`); an adapter sets this to brand its shortcuts (spt-claude-code → `cc`, giving `cc-<id>`). Additive + N-1-safe (omitted from serialization when absent). The picker reads it from the RESOLVED manifest of the selected adapter.

AdapterKind.description: The two adapter kinds. A `harness` hosts agents; a `shell` provides a driven surface (MANIFEST §Shell adapters).

Hook.description: `[hooks.<event>]` — one harness event → the `api` command it fires, the stdin fields it maps, and whether it can surface context to the agent.

fires.description: Opaque `api …` command line the harness invokes for this event.

can_inject.description: Whether this hook can inject context (false ⇒ sentinel/relay fallback).

Session.description: `[session]` — the watched-dir keys (`commune_dir`/`signoff_dir`) co-located with the fixed set of `[session.<role>]` command templates.

notif.description: `[session.notif]` — the endpoint-native notification render (ADR-0007's `notif_command` seam, REQ-NOTIF-2): an OS toast, a GameRobot `alert-symbol`, anything the adapter can run. Spawned detached when a notif surfaces at this endpoint, combinable with the agent-surface delivery. Keys spt-core fills: `{notif_id}`, `{notif_from}`, `{notif_subnet}`, `{notif_body}`.

SessionRole.description: `[session.<role>]` — one opaque outbound command template plus its spawn context. Model/tools/flags all live inside `command`, never as fields (MANIFEST §session roles). No nested tables here (keeps TOML round-trip emission scalar-before-table clean).

command.description: Opaque command line, with `{key}` substitution placeholders.

recursion_guard_env.description: Env var set on summarizer children so their own hooks bail (recursion guard).

env_remove.description: Env vars to strip from the child's inherited environment.

keys.description: Substitution keys spt-core guarantees to fill for this role.

value.description: Value to inject (with substitution); required for `inject`.

channel.description: Harness-hosted injection channel (spt-hosted inherits from the broker).

fetcher.description: `fetcher` strategy: adapter binary emitting normalized history.

locate_template.description: `locate_normalize` strategy: where the raw transcript lives.

normalize_command.description: `locate_normalize` strategy: command normalizing the raw transcript.

oneOf.description: spt-core asks the adapter (pull, adapter binary emits normalized).
spt-core locates the raw transcript then normalizes it.
Adapter pushes via `api history-log`; spt-core stores (Path-B).

Digest.description: `[digest]` — the session-digest extractor seam (ADR-0019). Reverses M9's "no manifest seam" stance: the digest gets its **own** adapter-declared extractor, distinct from `[history]` (which stays opaque + single-session and feeds the echo-commune verbatim). The extractor maps the harness's **native** log → the published `{role, text, tool, ts}` digest-record contract ([`spt_term::DigestRecord`]). **Imperative, not a DSL** (ADR-0019 §Decision): real harness logs are nested (one line → many entries, mixed block lists, types to filter) — a flat declarative map cannot express them, and a map powerful enough is a reinvented language. So the extractor is an opaque command spt-core never parses, exactly like every other manifest template. **Source.** By default the extractor reads the **same files as `[history]`** (the `locate_template`; DRY). An adapter may override with `source` (the own-source escape hatch). `api digest-entry` push remains the always-available fallback for a log-less adapter (which declares no `[digest]` at all). **Presentation.** `window_turns`, `arg_truncation`, and `sprint_collapse` are adapter-declared **defaults** any consumer may override at pull/subscribe; spt-core ships fallback defaults ([`spt_term::DigestConfig`]) when absent. The fixed "~3 turns" is no longer an spt-core requirement (ADR-0019).

extractor.description: Opaque extractor command: native harness log → the `{role,text,tool,ts}` contract (one JSON record per output line). `{key}` substitution applies (e.g. `{session_id}`, and `{source}` for the resolved log path).

source.description: Own-source escape hatch: a `locate_template` for the log file(s) the extractor reads. Absent ⇒ reuse `[history].locate_template` (DRY).

window_turns.description: Adapter-default window depth (user turns kept). Absent ⇒ spt-core fallback.

arg_truncation.description: Adapter-default tool-arg truncation width. Absent ⇒ spt-core fallback.

sprint_collapse.description: Adapter-default for collapsing consecutive tool records into one sprint. Absent ⇒ spt-core fallback (collapse on).

Identity.description: `[identity]` — how the harness's session id is obtained.

parent_ancestor_name.description: Process-tree anchor name when `session_id` is absent.

oneOf.description: Injected as a UUID the harness echoes back.

Update.description: `[update]` — adapter self-update directive (parsed in M2a; conducted in M3).

command.description: `delegated` avenue: the command spt-core delegates to.

signing_key.description: `file_pull` avenue: the adapter's Ed25519 **content-signing public key** (64 hex chars / 32 bytes). spt-core verifies a pulled payload against this per-adapter key before applying it (REQ-UPD-5 adapter content signing, ADR-0004 §D) — the adapter author signs their own releases; spt-core's release key stays scoped to spt-core. **Required for `file_pull`** (there are bytes to verify); not applicable to `delegated` (opaque updater).

self_verifies.description: `delegated` avenue: the adapter attests its own updater verifies the content it installs (e.g. `claude.exe plugin update` checks its own signatures). spt-core cannot see a delegated updater's bytes, so it delegates the trust **only** when this is set; an unattested delegated update is skipped as unverifiable (REQ-UPD-5).

uninstall.description: Optional inverse of install — run by `spt adapter remove` once the adapter is quiesced (the mirror of `spt adapter add`, which reuses this section as the install mechanism). Absent ⇒ spt-core's default cleanup. (Modeled in M2a; conducted with adapter-registration later.)

oneOf.description: Delegate to the adapter's own updater (e.g. `claude plugin update`).
spt-core pulls files from a repo.

Shell.description: `[shell]` — the body of a `kind = "shell"` adapter (a driven surface).

ephemeral.description: Ephemeral ⇒ no offline perch + no history retention.

command_receipt.description: How the shell receives agent commands.

pre_close.description: Instruction sent to the binary on link-break.

persistent.description: Auto-online whenever the owner endpoint is online.

wake_command.description: Long-running wake-watcher run WHILE offline; exit ⇒ revive.

can_shutdown.description: Whether the shell may fire `api owner-shutdown` to suspend its owner.

require_approval.description: Per-spawn user approval gate (floor; a node/endpoint setting may tighten). Absent ⇒ `none`. (Modeled now; conducted when shells land.)

max_instances_per_owner.description: Optional ceiling on concurrent-existing instances per owner endpoint (online + offline both count). Absent ⇒ unlimited.

over_cap.description: What happens at the cap: `reject` (default) or `approve` (per-spawn approval beyond the cap; does not raise it). Only meaningful with a cap.

ShellApproval.description: Per-shell instantiation-approval mode (`require_approval`). Reuses the consent plumbing: `remembered` lets allow-always write a persistent grant; `always` suppresses allow-always (prompt every spawn).

oneOf.description: No approval (default — matches the system's everything-opt-in posture).
Prompt; allow-always persists a grant, later spawns auto-allow.
Prompt on every spawn; allow-always suppressed (no persistent grant).

OverCap.description: What happens when an owner is at its `max_instances_per_owner` cap.

oneOf.description: Require per-spawn approval beyond the cap (does not raise the cap).

ShellCapability.description: One entry in `[shell.capabilities]` — a command and its argument names.

Sensory.description: `[shell.sensory]` — the sensory payload types a shell may emit.

Hint.description: `[[hints]]` — one once-per-session keyword hint (CONTEXT.md §keyword hints). The adapter's user-prompt hook pipes the full user message to `spt api hint`; a matching keyword surfaces `text` to the agent's context channel, at most once per session and once per message.

keywords.description: Keywords that fire the hint — literal **case-insensitive substrings** by default; compiled as **regex** patterns when `regex = true`.

text.description: The hint text surfaced when a keyword matches.

regex.description: Treat `keywords` as regex patterns instead of literal substrings.
